title: no-mistakes
description: A local git proxy that sits in front of your remote: push to it instead of origin and it spins up a disposable worktree, runs an AI validation pipeline (review, test, docs, lint), auto-fixes safe issues, escalates the rest, and opens a clean PR only after every check passes.

title: Paraform Talent Density Index
description: Paraform ranks companies not by funding or logos but by how badly the market wants their people — an algorithm over hiring data across thousands of roles and millions of candidate profiles. Thinking Machines, OpenAI, and Anthropic top the list.

title: 32 Ways Your Writing Looks Like an AI Wrote It
description: Adam Dunkels: 32 recurring patterns that mark text as AI-written — em-dash overuse, hedging, filler phrases, uniform sentences — plus tools to spot and strip them from your own writing.

title: Hermes Agent for QA Automation
description: Arghajit Singha on Hermes: a terminal-native AI agent for QA workflows that runs against local LLMs, holds persistent memory of your test environment, and turns repetitive shell commands into reusable skills.

title: Building an MCP Ecosystem at Pinterest
description: Pinterest built a production ecosystem of domain-specific MCP servers behind a central registry, saving an estimated 7,000 engineering hours per month across 66,000 monthly invocations.

title: Introducing the MCP Registry
description: Preview of an open catalog and API for discovering publicly available MCP servers, with room for public and private sub-registries to build on top.

title: Impeccable
description: Paul Bakaus: a design-fluency skill for AI coding agents — teaches Claude and others real design vocabulary and patterns so they stop generating purple gradients, nested cards, and other AI slop.

title: Taste Skill
description: Leon Lin and blueemi: an open-source design-system framework that gives AI coding agents the rules and templates to ship polished, coherent frontends instead of generic AI output.

title: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness
description: LangChain: a breakdown of the harness layer that wraps an LLM into a working agent — the loop, tools, memory, and control flow that turn raw model calls into reliable autonomous behavior.

title: The Bus Ticket Theory of Genius
description: Paul Graham: great work requires natural ability, determination, and a disinterested obsession with something that matters — an intense, purposeless interest that lets you follow unpromising paths others overlook.

title: Before the Startup
description: Paul Graham: the best preparation for founding a startup is pursuing genuine intellectual curiosity and interesting problems, not attempting to launch ventures while still in school.

title: Why Startup Founders Should Launch Sooner Than They Think
description: Y Combinator: the cost of launching too early is lower than you think, and the cost of waiting is higher.

title: The New Way To Build A Startup
description: Y Combinator: how AI is collapsing the team size needed to build a company from the ground up.

title: How to Spend Your 20s in the AI Era
description: Y Combinator: where to focus your energy when AI is compressing the value of credentials and raising the ceiling on what one person can build.

title: My AI Adoption Journey
description: Mitchell Hashimoto's six-step journey from dismissing AI to running agents continuously in the background.

title: Your AI Agent Doesn't Care About Your README
description: Reya Vir: Auto-generate a hierarchy of AGENTS.md files so your AI agent gets dense, folder-specific context instead of one giant README.

title: Using GraphViz for CLAUDE.md
description: Trigger-based GraphViz flowcharts help Claude follow instructions more reliably than markdown rules.

title: gstack
description: Garry Tan's Claude Code setup: 23 opinionated skills covering CEO, designer, eng manager, release manager, and QA roles.

title: Cities and Ambition
description: Different cities send different messages. New York says you should make more money. Cambridge says you should be smarter.
